description: A categorized settings panel using the Navigation Settings component with grouped sections. Each section contains controls like Switch for toggles, Select for choices, and RadioGroup for exclusive options. Includes Save/Cancel buttons and an unsaved changes warning.
use_cases: application-wide settings page with grouped preferences (appearance, notifications, privacy)
user account settings with profile, security, and notification sections
project or workspace configuration panel with categorized options
admin panel settings with multiple pages of options organized by category

sections.when: You need a full settings page with multiple categories/pages of options, where each category contains labeled settings with different control types (toggles, dropdowns, radio buttons). The Navigation Settings component provides built-in page navigation, filtering, and layout.
sections.not_for: A single form with a few fields (use the form-with-validation recipe). Feature flags managed by a backend system with no user-facing UI. Quick toggles in a toolbar or header (use Switch or DropdownMenu directly). A wizard-like guided setup (use the multi-step-wizard recipe).

steps: npm install @gravity-ui/navigation @gravity-ui/uikit
Wrap your app in ThemeProvider from @gravity-ui/uikit if not already done

items.name: Settings
items.role: Root settings layout component with built-in page navigation sidebar, search/filter bar, and content area. Uses Settings.Group for category grouping, Settings.Page for individual pages, and Settings.Section with Settings.Item for individual settings within a page. Props: title, initialPage, onPageChange, filterPlaceholder, loading, onClose.

items.name: Switch
items.role: Toggle control for boolean on/off settings (e.g. enable notifications, dark mode). Props: checked, onUpdate, size, disabled, content (label text).

items.name: Select
items.role: Dropdown for choosing from predefined options (e.g. language, timezone, default view). Props: value, onUpdate, options, placeholder, width, filterable for long lists.

items.name: RadioGroup
items.role: Exclusive choice selector for settings with 2-5 mutually exclusive options (e.g. theme: light/dark/system, date format). Props: value, onUpdate, options, direction ('horizontal' or 'vertical'), size.

items.name: Button
items.role: Save and Cancel action buttons in the settings footer. Use view='action' for Save, view='outlined' for Cancel. Props: view, size, disabled (when no changes), loading (during save), onClick.

items.name: Divider
items.role: Visual separator between settings sections within a page. Use orientation='horizontal' between groups of related settings.

items.name: Text
items.role: Section headings, setting labels, and description text. Use variant='subheader-2' for section titles, variant='body-1' for labels, variant='caption-1' with color='secondary' for helper text.

items.name: useSettingsState hook
items.description: Manages settings values, tracks changes against the saved state, and provides save/reset functionality.
items.approach: Custom hook that takes initial settings as the saved baseline. Stores current values in state. Exposes hasChanges (deep comparison of current vs. saved), update(key, value), save() to persist and update baseline, and reset() to revert current to saved values.

items.name: Unsaved changes guard
items.description: Warns the user when navigating away or closing the settings panel with unsaved changes.
items.approach: Use a beforeunload event listener and optionally a ConfirmDialog when the user clicks the close button or navigates away while hasChanges is true. Prompt with 'You have unsaved changes. Discard them?' with Discard and Stay buttons.

tree: RadioGroup  (value, onUpdate, options=[light, dark, system], direction='horizontal')
RadioGroup  (value, onUpdate, options=[immediate, daily, weekly], direction='vertical')
div.settings-footer  (sticky bottom bar, flex row, gap=8)
flow: Settings renders with initialPage selected in the sidebar. Search bar filters pages by title.
User navigates between pages via the sidebar -> onPageChange fires, active page content renders
User toggles a Switch, selects a Select option, or changes a RadioGroup -> update(key, value) in settings state
hasChanges becomes true -> Save button enables, Cancel button enables
User clicks Save -> save() sends settings to API (loading state on button), on success baseline updates, hasChanges resets to false
User clicks Cancel -> reset() reverts all values to saved baseline, hasChanges becomes false
User clicks close (onClose) or navigates away while hasChanges is true -> ConfirmDialog warns about unsaved changes
User confirms discard -> close/navigate. User cancels -> stay on settings page.

sections.title: Settings page with appearance, notifications, and privacy sections

items: Building a custom sidebar navigation for settings pages when Settings from @gravity-ui/navigation provides page navigation, search filtering, and grouping out of the box.
Saving each setting immediately on change without a Save button -- this makes it impossible to discard changes and can cause unexpected side effects. Batch changes and save explicitly.
Using Checkbox instead of Switch for boolean toggles -- Switch is the standard control for on/off settings; Checkbox implies multi-select or form agreement.
Nesting settings too deeply (more than Group > Page > Section) -- keep the hierarchy flat for discoverability. Two levels of grouping is sufficient.
Forgetting the unsaved changes warning when the user navigates away -- always check hasChanges before closing or leaving the settings panel.
Placing Save/Cancel buttons inside each section instead of a single footer -- creates confusion about what gets saved. Use one pair of action buttons for the entire settings form.
Using RadioGroup for settings with more than 5 options -- switch to Select for longer lists to save vertical space and improve scannability.

items.note: Use a ConfirmDialog for the unsaved changes warning instead of window.confirm for a polished experience.
If individual settings require validation (e.g. custom domain, API key format), apply the per-field validation pattern from form-with-validation.
Show a loading state via the Settings loading prop while initial settings data is being fetched from the API.

title: Spacing and Containers
description: Control element spacing (padding and margin) with Box and constrain page-level width with Container. Replaces inline styles for spacing with design-token-based Space values.
use_cases: add consistent padding to a section wrapper using design tokens
center page content with a max-width constraint
use semantic HTML elements (section, article, nav, main) with design-system spacing
apply shorthand spacing (all sides, axis, or individual side) via a single prop

sections.when: You need to control the spacing of a single element (padding/margin) or constrain the width of a page section. Box is the spacing primitive; Container is the page-width wrapper.
sections.not_for: Gaps between sibling elements (use gap prop on Flex or space prop on Row). Column-based grid layouts (use Row+Col). Sidebar/navigation shells (use page-layout recipe).

matrix.situation: Section needs uniform padding
matrix.why: Box spacing prop accepts {p: '4'} to apply padding on all sides using a design token value.

matrix.situation: Page content should be centered and not exceed a max width
matrix.why: Container centers content with auto margins and applies a predefined max-width from the breakpoint scale.

matrix.situation: Need a <section> or <article> element with spacing
matrix.why: The as prop makes Box polymorphic — it renders the chosen HTML element while still applying spacing tokens.

items.name: Box
items.role: Spacing primitive. Props: spacing (SpacingProps: {p, px, py, pt, pr, pb, pl, m, mx, my, mt, mr, mb, ml} — all values are Space tokens '0'|'0.5'|'1'..'10'), overflow ('hidden'|'x'|'y'|'auto'), width/height/maxWidth/maxHeight/minWidth/minHeight (CSS strings), position (CSS position), as (polymorphic HTML tag or component).

items.name: Container
items.role: Page-width constraint. Centers content horizontally with auto margins. Props: maxWidth (MediaType breakpoint name: 'xs'|'s'|'m'|'l'|'xl'|'xxl'|'xxxl'), gutters (Space token or false — controls horizontal padding inside the container), spaceRow (Space token or MediaPartial<Space> — gap between Row children), as (HTML tag).

sections.title: Box as semantic section element with padding
Container centering page content with max-width
Box spacing shorthand for card internal layout

items: Using inline style={{padding: '...'}} for spacing — use Box spacing prop to stay on design token values
Passing pixel values to spacing — spacing only accepts Space tokens ('0', '0.5', '1'..'10'), not arbitrary CSS strings
Nesting multiple Containers on the same page — use one Container at the outermost level per content region; nesting Containers double-applies max-width constraints
Using Container maxWidth with a pixel string — maxWidth accepts breakpoint names ('xs', 's', 'm', 'l', 'xl', 'xxl', 'xxxl'), not CSS pixel values
Using Box where Flex gap is cleaner — if you only need spacing between sibling elements, Flex gap is more semantic

items.note: Use Flex gap for spacing between siblings; use Box spacing for padding/margin on a single element.
Wrap Row+Col layouts in a Container to constrain total page width and add gutters.
Container is the direct child of AsideHeader's renderContent, constraining the content region width.

title: Theming and Dark Mode Setup
description: Foundation recipe for configuring Gravity UI theming, wrapping your app in ThemeProvider, and building a theme toggle that supports light, dark, and system-preference modes.
use_cases: set up Gravity UI theming from scratch in a new project
add a dark mode toggle to an existing application
respect the user's OS-level color scheme preference
scope a different theme to a specific section of the page

sections.when: You are starting a new Gravity UI project, or adding theme switching to an existing one. ThemeProvider must wrap all Gravity UI components — it injects the CSS custom properties (--g-color-*) that every component depends on.
sections.not_for: Per-component color overrides (use className or style props instead). Branding that only changes the accent color (override --g-color-base-brand in CSS). Server-side-only pages with no client React tree.

steps: npm install @gravity-ui/uikit
Import the required CSS at the top of your entry file: import '@gravity-ui/uikit/styles/fonts.css'; import '@gravity-ui/uikit/styles/styles.css';
Wrap your root component in ThemeProvider: import {ThemeProvider} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';
Set the theme prop to 'light', 'dark', or 'system' — 'system' follows the user's prefers-color-scheme media query
Use the useTheme() hook anywhere inside the provider to read or change the current theme at runtime

items.name: ThemeProvider
items.role: Root provider that injects CSS custom properties (--g-color-base-background, --g-color-text-primary, etc.) onto a wrapper element. Accepts theme prop: 'light' | 'dark' | 'system'. Can be nested for scoped theming.

items.name: useTheme
items.role: Hook returning [theme, setTheme]. theme is the resolved value ('light' | 'dark'). setTheme accepts 'light' | 'dark' | 'system'.

items.name: Switch
items.role: Simple two-state toggle, suitable for a light/dark toggle when system mode is not offered. Props: checked, onUpdate, size ('s' | 'm' | 'l').

items.name: RadioGroup
items.role: Three-option selector for light / dark / system. Props: options (RadioGroupOption[]), value, onUpdate, direction ('horizontal' | 'vertical'), size ('m' | 'l' | 'xl').

items.name: ThemeToggle (Switch variant)
items.description: A composed component that uses Switch to flip between light and dark. Reads current theme from useTheme() and calls setTheme on toggle.

items.name: ThemeSelector (RadioGroup variant)
items.description: A composed component that uses RadioGroup to choose between light, dark, and system. The system option defers to the OS prefers-color-scheme.

tree: Main content  (all Gravity UI components inherit theme)

sections.title: Full app setup with system theme and toggle
Scoped dark section inside a light app
Persisting theme preference to localStorage

items: Toggling a .dark-theme CSS class on the body manually -- ThemeProvider manages the CSS custom properties; duplicating this leads to conflicts and flickering
Importing individual component CSS files instead of the main styles.css -- the main stylesheet includes all component styles and theme token definitions
Hardcoding color hex values instead of using --g-color-* tokens -- hardcoded values will not respond to theme changes
Using window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)') directly -- ThemeProvider with theme='system' handles this automatically, including listening for changes
Wrapping every component in its own ThemeProvider -- one root provider is sufficient; nest only when you need a scoped theme override
Forgetting the CSS imports (fonts.css, styles.css) -- components will render without any styles or theme tokens

items.note: Dialogs inherit the current theme from ThemeProvider automatically.
Toaster inherits theme context; no extra setup needed for themed notifications.

title: User Feedback: Toasts, Alerts, and Notifications
description: A molecule recipe for providing user feedback through three complementary channels: Toaster for transient success/error messages after actions, Alert for persistent inline warnings and info, and Notifications for a notification center with history.
use_cases: show a success toast after saving, deleting, or submitting data
display an error toast when an API call fails with a retry action
render persistent inline alerts for warnings, deprecation notices, or required actions
build a notification center (bell icon) showing recent activity and unread items
provide non-blocking feedback after bulk operations complete

sections.when: Your application needs to communicate outcomes of user actions (success, error), display persistent warnings or information, or maintain a notification history that users can review later.
sections.not_for: Form field validation errors (use TextInput validationState and errorMessage). Confirmation before an action (use ConfirmDialog). Loading states (use Loader or Skeleton). Tooltips for UI explanation (use Popover or HelpMark).

matrix.situation: Action completed successfully or failed (transient, auto-dismissing)
matrix.why: Toaster shows a brief non-blocking message that auto-hides after a timeout. Ideal for fire-and-forget feedback like 'Saved successfully' or 'Delete failed'.

matrix.situation: Persistent warning or info that requires user attention until dismissed
matrix.why: Alert renders inline and stays visible until the user closes it or the condition resolves. Suitable for deprecation notices, permission warnings, or required configuration steps.

matrix.situation: Notification history with timestamps, read/unread state, and a bell icon trigger
matrix.why: Notifications from @gravity-ui/components provides a full notification center with list rendering, empty/error/loading states, and infinite scroll.

steps: npm install @gravity-ui/uikit @gravity-ui/components
Wrap your app in ThemeProvider from @gravity-ui/uikit if not already done
Add ToasterProvider at the app root (wraps children and renders the toast container): import {ToasterProvider, ToasterComponent} from '@gravity-ui/uikit'
Render <ToasterComponent /> inside the provider (typically at the end of the provider children)

items.name: Toaster
items.role: Transient toast notifications. Use the useToaster() hook to call add() with name (unique ID), title, content, theme ('success'|'danger'|'warning'|'info'), autoHiding (ms or false), isClosable, and actions (buttons inside the toast). Requires ToasterProvider and ToasterComponent in the app root.

items.name: Alert
items.role: Persistent inline notification. Accepts theme ('info'|'success'|'warning'|'danger'), title, message, view ('filled'|'outlined'), onClose for dismissibility, and actions for action buttons. Renders in-place within the page layout.

items.name: Notifications
items.role: Full notification center component displaying a list of NotificationProps items with id, content, formattedDate, and optional actions. Supports isLoading, emptyTitle, emptyContent, errorTitle, and onLoadMoreNotifications for infinite scroll.

items.name: Notification bell trigger
items.description: A button with a bell icon and unread count badge that opens the Notifications panel in a Popup.
items.approach: Render a Button with Icon data={Bell} and a Badge or Label showing the unread count. On click, toggle a Popup anchored to the button containing the Notifications component. Track unread count from your notification state/API.

items.name: Toast helper functions
items.description: Convenience wrappers around useToaster().add() for common feedback patterns.
items.approach: Create a useFeedback() hook that returns showSuccess(title, content?), showError(title, content?), and showWarning(title, content?) functions. Each calls add() with appropriate theme, autoHiding (e.g. 5000ms for success, false for errors), and isClosable settings.

tree: Alert?  (inline, persistent warning/info banner at top of page)
... components that call useToaster().add() on actions ...
flow: 1. App mounts -> ToasterProvider wraps app, ToasterComponent renders toast container
2. User performs action (save, delete, submit) -> handler calls useToaster().add({name, title, theme})
3. Toast appears in corner, auto-hides after autoHiding ms (default 5000) or stays if autoHiding=false
4. User clicks toast close button (isClosable) or action button -> toast dismissed, callback fired
5. For persistent feedback: Alert renders inline with theme='warning', message, and optional onClose
6. User dismisses Alert via close button -> onClose callback hides alert from state
7. Notification bell shows unread count badge -> user clicks -> Popup opens with Notifications list
8. Notifications renders items with formattedDate, content, and per-notification actions
9. User scrolls to bottom -> onLoadMoreNotifications fetches older notifications

sections.title: Toast feedback after save/delete actions with helper hook
Inline Alert for persistent warnings and a notification bell with Popup

items: Using Toaster for persistent messages that require user action -- toasts auto-hide and are easy to miss; use Alert for persistent inline feedback
Using Alert for transient success messages -- Alert stays in the layout until dismissed; use Toaster for fire-and-forget success notifications
Showing multiple toasts for the same event by using the same name -- Toaster deduplicates by name, so use unique names (e.g. append timestamp) for repeated events
Forgetting to add ToasterProvider and ToasterComponent at the app root -- useToaster() will throw without the provider context
Building a custom notification dropdown from scratch -- Notifications from @gravity-ui/components handles list rendering, empty/error states, loading, and infinite scroll
Displaying raw error stack traces in toasts -- show a user-friendly title and message, log the full error to the console or monitoring service
Using window.alert() for any feedback -- it blocks the main thread, cannot be styled, and provides a poor user experience

items.note: Use a confirmation dialog before destructive actions, then show a toast after the action completes.
Combine Alert with page error states -- show an inline Alert for recoverable errors and PlaceholderContainer for full-page failures.
Place the notification bell in the app shell header and render inline Alerts at the top of the content area.
